page: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 1229 GB of capacity, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the average tier for standard compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, fall within the middle 50% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth for both small cached blocks (416.32 GB/sec) and large uncached blocks (44.52 GB/sec read, 31.87 GB/sec write) also registers in the average range. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, and medium-sized 7B LLM inference speed is poor to weak, indicating limitations for complex parallel decompression and larger AI workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-8ds_v4 offers a high density of memory and local storage relative to its active vCPU count. This makes it a resource-dense option for workloads that require large memory footprints but do not scale linearly with CPU core counts, avoiding the cost of licensing or paying for unneeded vCPUs. The primary tradeoff is the lower compute density, as demonstrated by the average to weak multi-threaded benchmark results.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, database caching layers, relational databases requiring high RAM-to-CPU ratios, and local storage-dependent workloads. It is also appropriate for static web serving and Redis deployments, where it achieves average throughput, but is less suitable for compute-heavy parallel processing or medium-to-large LLM inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the EDSv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage. This configuration provides an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard single-core and multi-core CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations. However, multi-threaded decompression and medium-model LLM inference show weaker performance. Qualitatively, the instance offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the cost of excess vCPUs. It is best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and local storage-dependent applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v4 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering average benchmark performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 8 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high RAM-to-CPU allocation and local storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB per core
1229 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in standard CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis benchmarks
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and static web serving
